name: Carmustine
description: A cell-cycle phase nonspecific alkylating antineoplastic agent. It is used in the treatment of brain tumors and various other malignant neoplasms. (From Martindale, The Extra Pharmacopoeia, 30th ed, p462) This substance may reasonably be anticipated to be a carcinogen according to the Fourth Annual Report on Carcinogens (NTP 85-002, 1985). (From Merck Index, 11th ed)
indication: For the treatment of brain tumors, multiple myeloma, Hodgkin's disease and Non-Hodgkin's lymphomas.
toxicity: The oral LD<sub>50</sub>s in rat and mouse are 20 mg/kg and 45 mg/kg, respectively. Side effects include leukopenia, thrombocytopenia, nausea. Toxic effects include pulmonary fibrosis (20-0%) and bone marrow toxicity.
mechanismOfAction: Carmustine causes cross-links in DNA and RNA, leading to the inhibition of DNA synthesis, RNA production and RNA translation (protein synthesis). Carmustine also binds to and modifies (carbamoylates) glutathione reductase. This leads to cell death.
pharmacodynamics: Carmustine is one of the nitrosoureas indicated as palliative therapy as a single agent or in established combination therapy with other approved chemotherapeutic agents in treatment of brain tumors, multiple myeloma, Hodgkin's disease, and non-Hodgkin's lymphomas. Although it is generally agreed that carmustine alkylates DNA and RNA, it is not cross resistant with other alkylators. As with other nitrosoureas, it may also inhibit several key enzymatic processes by carbamoylation of amino acids in proteins.

interactions.name: Metamizole
interactions.description: The risk or severity of myelosuppression can be increased when Metamizole is combined with Carmustine.

interactions.name: Natalizumab
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Carmustine is combined with Natalizumab.

interactions.name: Pimecrolimus
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Pimecrolimus is combined with Carmustine.

interactions.name: Tofacitinib
interactions.description: Carmustine may increase the immunosuppressive activities of Tofacitinib.

interactions.name: Tacrolimus
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Tacrolimus is combined with Carmustine.

interactions.name: Clozapine
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Carmustine is combined with Clozapine.

interactions.name: BCG vaccine
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of BCG vaccine can be decreased when used in combination with Carmustine.

interactions.name: Rabies virus inactivated antigen, A
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Carmustine is combined with Rabies virus inactivated antigen, A.

interactions.name: Cimetidine
interactions.description: Cimetidine may increase the myelosuppressive activities of Carmustine.

interactions.name: Vemurafenib
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Carmustine can be increased when it is combined with Vemurafenib.

interactions.name: Trastuzumab
interactions.description: Trastuzumab may increase the cardiotoxic activities of Carmustine.

interactions.name: Fluvoxamine
